title: Setting: hide type filter from toolbar
description:
Add a configuration option that removes the type selector (Initiatives/Epics/Issues AnimatedSelector) from the toolbar entirely. This is for users who prefer a less crowded toolbar or who primarily use the type hierarchy section in the filter sidebar. Hiding the toolbar control does NOT hide or disable the type hierarchy section in the filter sidebar.

Scenario: Show type filter in toolbar setting is on by default

Given a fresh installation with no custom configuration
When the user views the toolbar
Then the Initiatives/Epics/Issues type selector is visible in the toolbar

Scenario: User turns off Show type filter in toolbar

Given the settings sidebar is open
When the user turns off the Show type filter in toolbar toggle
Then the type selector AnimatedSelector is removed from the toolbar
And the toolbar is narrower
And the type hierarchy section in the filter sidebar is still present and functional

Scenario: Type filter in toolbar setting is independent of sidebar type section

Given the user has turned off Show type filter in toolbar
When the user opens the filter sidebar
Then the type hierarchy section is still visible in the filter sidebar
And the user can still filter by type using the sidebar controls

Scenario: Hiding toolbar type filter does not affect active type filter state

Given Show type filter in toolbar is on
And the type selector has Epics selected
When the user turns off Show type filter in toolbar
Then the type filter remains active (Epics is still filtered)
And the same filter state is reflected in the filter sidebar type section

Scenario: Show type filter in toolbar setting persists across reload

Given the user has turned off Show type filter in toolbar
When the page is reloaded
Then the type selector remains hidden from the toolbar
creator: ryan.porter
